{"step_by_step_thinking": "The primary lower 1st molar (tooth S, L) is described as having four cusps: two buccal and two lingual, including the distobuccal, distolingual, mesiolingual, and mesiobuccal cusps. This description is quite specific and unique compared to the other options. The primary lower 2nd molar (tooth T, K) is described as resembling the first mandibular molar of the permanent teeth, which is less unique. The primary upper 1st molar (tooth B, I) and the primary upper 2nd molar (tooth A, J) are described as resembling the permanent maxillary first molar, which is also less unique. Therefore, the primary lower 1st molar stands out as the most atypical (unique) tooth.", "answer_choice": "A"}
